    14â†’limitations under the License.
    15â†’-/
    16â†’import Mathlib.NumberTheory.LegendreSymbol.JacobiSymbol
    17â†’
    18â†’
    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Wall-Sun-Sun primes
    21â†’
    22â†’*References:*
    23â†’- [Wikipedia, Lucas_sequence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucas_sequence)
    24â†’- [Wikipedia, Wallâ€“Sunâ€“Sun prime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wall%E2%80%93Sun%E2%80%93Sun_prime)
    25â†’- [Wikipedia, Wieferich prime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucas%E2%80%93Wieferich_prime)
    26â†’-/
    27â†’
    28â†’open scoped NumberTheorySymbols
    29â†’
    30â†’/--
    31â†’**The Lucas sequence of the first kind**
    32â†’$U_0(P, Q) = 0$, $U_1(P, Q)=1$, $U_{n+2}(P, Q)=PU_{n+1}(P, Q)-QU_n(P, Q)$
    33â†’-/
    34â†’def LucasSequence.U (P Q : â„¤) : â„• â†’ â„¤
    35â†’  | 0 => 0
    36â†’  | 1 => 1
    37â†’  | n + 2 => P * LucasSequence.U P Q (n + 1) - Q * LucasSequence.U P Q n
    38â†’
    39â†’/--
    40â†’**The Lucas sequence of the second kind**
    41â†’$V_0(P, Q) = 0$, $V_1(P, Q)=P$, $V_{n+2}(P, Q)=PV_{n+1}(P, Q)-QV_n(P, Q)$
    42â†’-/
    43â†’def LucasSequence.V (P Q : â„¤) : â„• â†’ â„¤
    44â†’  | 0 => 2
    45â†’  | 1 => P
    46â†’  | n + 2 => P * LucasSequence.V P Q (n + 1) - Q * LucasSequence.V P Q n
    47â†’
    48â†’/--
    49â†’**The Lucas numbers**
    50â†’$L_0 = 2$, $L_1=1$, $L_{n+2} = L_{n+1}+L_n$
    51â†’-/
    52â†’def lucasNumber : â„• â†’ â„¤ := LucasSequence.V 1 (-1)
    53â†’
    54â†’/--
    55â†’**Wallâ€“Sunâ€“Sun prime**
    56â†’A prime $p$ is a Wallâ€“Sunâ€“Sun prime if and only if $L_p \equiv 1 \pmod{p^2}$, where $L_p$ is
    57â†’the $p$-th Lucas number.
    58â†’-/
    59â†’structure IsWallSunSunPrime (p : â„•) : Prop where
    60â†’  prime : p.Prime
    61â†’  lucasNumber_modeq : lucasNumber p â‰، 1 [ZMOD (p ^ 2)]
    62â†’
    63â†’/--
    64â†’**Lucasâ€“Wieferich prime**
    65â†’A Lucasâ€“Wieferich prime associated with $(a,b)$ is an odd prime $p$, not dividing $a^2 - 4b$, such
    66â†’that $U_{p-\varepsilon}(a,b) \equiv 0 \pmod{p^2}$ where $U(a,b)$ is the Lucas sequence of the first
    67â†’kind and $\varepsilon$ is the Legendre symbol $\left({\tfrac {a^{2}-4b}{p}}\right)$.
    68â†’
    69â†’See first paragraph of
    70â†’https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wall%E2%80%93Sun%E2%80%93Sun_prime#Wall%E2%80%93Sun%E2%80%93Sun_primes_with_discriminant_D
    71â†’for the condition that $p$ is odd and coprime to the discriminant
    72â†’-/
    73â†’structure IsLucasWieferichPrime (a b p : â„•) : Prop where
    74â†’  prime : p.Prime
    75â†’  odd : Odd p
    76â†’  not_dvd : آ¬(p : â„¤) âˆ£ a ^ 2 - 4 * b
    77â†’  modeq : LucasSequence.U a b (p - J(a^2 - 4*b | p)).toNat â‰، 0 [ZMOD (p^2)]
    78â†’

    35â†’/-- A multiplicative basis of some order. -/

    44â†’/-- The sum convolution of two functions `f, g : â„• â†’ R`, also known as the Cauchy product.

    17â†’import Mathlib.Data.Nat.PrimeFin
    18â†’import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Lattice
    19â†’
    20â†’namespace Nat
    21â†’
    22â†’/-- The greatest prime divisor of a natural number `n > 1`.
    23â†’
    24â†’Takes the junk value `0` for `n = 0` and `n = 1`.
    25â†’
    26â†’TODO Consider redefining this using the computable implementation:
    27â†’`n.primeFactorsList.getLastI`. -/
    28â†’noncomputable def maxPrimeFac (n : â„•) : â„• := sSup {p : â„• | p.Prime âˆ§ p âˆ£ n}
    29â†’
    30â†’@[simp]
    31â†’lemma maxPrimeFac_zero :
    32â†’    maxPrimeFac 0 = 0 := by
    33â†’  simpa [maxPrimeFac] using Set.Infinite.Nat.sSup_eq_zero infinite_setOf_prime
    34â†’
    35â†’@[simp]
    36â†’lemma maxPrimeFac_one :
    37â†’    maxPrimeFac 1 = 0 := by
    38â†’  suffices {p : â„• | p.Prime âˆ§ p = 1} = âˆ… by simp [maxPrimeFac, this]
    39â†’  aesop
    40â†’
    41â†’lemma prime_maxPrimeFac_of_one_lt (n : â„•) (h : 1 < n) :
    42â†’    Prime (maxPrimeFac n) := by
    43â†’  set s := {p : â„• | p.Prime âˆ§ p âˆ£ n} with hs
    44â†’  have hsâ‚€ : s.Nonempty := by
    45â†’    simp only [Set.Nonempty, Set.mem_setOf_eq, â†گ ne_one_iff_exists_prime_dvd, hs]
    46â†’    omega
    47â†’  have hsâ‚پ : BddAbove s := by
    48â†’    use n
    49â†’    simp only [hs, mem_upperBounds, Set.mem_setOf_eq, and_imp]
    50â†’    exact fun p _ hp â†¦ Nat.le_of_dvd (zero_lt_of_lt h) hp
    51â†’  exact (Nat.sSup_mem hsâ‚€ hsâ‚پ).1
    52â†’
    53â†’lemma maxPrimeFac_eq_of_dvd_of_le
    54â†’    (n p : â„•) (hn : 0 < n) (hp : p.Prime) (h_dvd : p âˆ£ n) (h_le : maxPrimeFac n â‰¤ p) :
    55â†’    maxPrimeFac n = p := by
    56â†’  refine le_antisymm h_le ?_
    57â†’  replace hn : 1 < n := Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le hp.one_lt (Nat.le_of_dvd hn h_dvd)
    58â†’  set s := {p : â„• | p.Prime âˆ§ p âˆ£ n} with hs
    59â†’  have hsâ‚پ : BddAbove s := by
    60â†’    use n
    61â†’    simp only [hs, mem_upperBounds, Set.mem_setOf_eq, and_imp]
    62â†’    exact fun p _ hp â†¦ Nat.le_of_dvd (zero_lt_of_lt hn) hp
    63â†’  exact ConditionallyCompleteLattice.le_csSup s p hsâ‚پ âں¨hp, h_dvdâں©
    64â†’
    65â†’@[simp]
    66â†’lemma one_lt_maxPrimeFac_iff (n : â„•) :
    67â†’    1 < maxPrimeFac n â†” 1 < n := by
    68â†’  rcases lt_trichotomy n 1 with hn | rfl | hn
    69â†’  آ· simp only [lt_one_iff] at hn
    70â†’    simp [hn]
    71â†’  آ· simp
    72â†’  آ· simpa [hn] using (prime_maxPrimeFac_of_one_lt n hn).one_lt
    73â†’
    74â†’end Nat

    17â†’import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Squarefree

    19â†’
    20â†’namespace Nat
    21â†’
    22â†’/-- `n.squarefreePart` is the unique `aâ‚€ : â„•` such that `aâ‚€` is squarefree and `n = aâ‚€ * b ^ 2`,
    23â†’for some `b : â„•`. At `0` this property is not well defined because `b` must be `0` and `aâ‚€` can be
    24â†’any squarefree number; we give the junk value `1` at `n = 0` following the convention that the
    25â†’squarefree part of any square is `1`. -/
    26â†’def squarefreePart (n : â„•) : â„• := n.factorization.prod fun (p e : â„•) â†¦ p ^ (e % 2)
    27â†’
    28â†’example : squarefreePart 2 = 2 := by
    29â†’  decide +native
    30â†’
    31â†’example : squarefreePart 5 = 5 := by
    32â†’  decide +native
    33â†’
    34â†’example : squarefreePart 4 = 1 := by
    35â†’  decide +native
    36â†’
    37â†’example : squarefreePart 8 = 2 := by
    38â†’  decide +native
    39â†’
    40â†’example : squarefreePart 16 = 1 := by
    41â†’  decide +native
    42â†’
    43â†’example : squarefreePart 24 = 6 := by
    44â†’  decide +native
    45â†’
    46â†’theorem squarefreePart_ne_zero (n : â„•) : n.squarefreePart â‰  0 := by
    47â†’  simp [squarefreePart]
    48â†’
    49â†’theorem squarefreePart_zero : squarefreePart 0 = 1 := by
    50â†’  simp [squarefreePart]
    51â†’
    52â†’/-- If `n` is squarefree, then its squarefree part is itself. -/
    53â†’theorem squarefreePart_of_squarefree {n : â„•} (hn : Squarefree n) :
    54â†’    squarefreePart n = n := by
    55â†’  nth_rw 2 [â†گ n.factorization_prod_pow_eq_self fun _ â†¦ by simp_all]
    56â†’  simp only [squarefreePart, Finsupp.prod, support_factorization]
    57â†’  exact Finset.prod_congr rfl fun p hp â†¦ by
    58â†’    rw [factorization_eq_one_of_squarefree hn (mem_primeFactors.1 hp).1 (mem_primeFactors.1 hp).2.1]
    59â†’
    60â†’/-- The squarefree part of any square is `1`. -/
    61â†’theorem squarefreePart_of_isSquare {n : â„•} (hn : IsSquare n) :
    62â†’    squarefreePart n = 1 := by
    63â†’  rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hâ‚€); exact squarefreePart_zero
    64â†’  obtain âں¨r, rflâں© := hn
    65â†’  rw [ne_eq, mul_eq_zero, or_self] at hâ‚€
    66â†’  rw [squarefreePart, Finsupp.prod, support_factorization,
    67â†’    Finset.prod_congr rfl fun p hp â†¦ by rw [r.factorization_mul hâ‚€ hâ‚€]]
    68â†’  simp [â†گ two_mul]
    69â†’
    70â†’/-- If $n = \prod_p p^{e_p}$ is the prime factorization of $n$, then $\prod_p p^{e_p \pmod{2}}$
    71â†’is the prime factorization of the squarefree part of $n$. -/
    72â†’theorem squarefreePart_factorization (n : â„•) {p : â„•} (hp : p.Prime) :
    73â†’    n.squarefreePart.factorization p = n.factorization p % 2 := by
    74â†’  rw [squarefreePart, prod_factorization_eq_prod_primeFactors,
    75â†’    n.prod_primeFactors_factorization_apply hp (f := fun p n â†¦ n.factorization p % 2)
    76â†’      (fun _ _ h â†¦ by simp [factorization_eq_zero_of_not_dvd h]) (by simp)]
    77â†’
    78â†’theorem Prime.squarefree {p : â„•} (hp : p.Prime) : Squarefree p := Irreducible.squarefree hp
    79â†’
    80â†’theorem squarefree_squarefreePart (n : â„•) : Squarefree n.squarefreePart := by
    81â†’  refine Nat.squarefree_iff_factorization_le_one n.squarefreePart_ne_zero |>.2 fun p â†¦ ?_
    82â†’  by_cases hp : p.Prime
    83â†’  آ· linarith [n.squarefreePart_factorization hp, Nat.mod_lt (n.factorization p) two_pos]
    84â†’  آ· linarith [factorization_eq_zero_of_not_prime n.squarefreePart hp]
    85â†’
    86â†’theorem squarefreePart_dvd (n : â„•) : squarefreePart n âˆ£ n := by
    87â†’  rcases eq_or_ne n 0 with (rfl | hâ‚€); simp
    88â†’  exact Nat.factorization_prime_le_iff_dvd n.squarefreePart_ne_zero hâ‚€ |>.1 fun p hp â†¦
    89â†’    squarefreePart_factorization _ hp â–¸ Nat.mod_le _ _
    90â†’
    91â†’/-- The square part is the value of `b ^ 2` in the squarefree decomposition of `n = aâ‚€ * b ^ 2`. -/
    92â†’def squarePart (n : â„•) : â„• := n / n.squarefreePart
    93â†’
    94â†’theorem squarePart_zero : squarePart 0 = 0 := by simp [squarePart]
    95â†’
    96â†’/-- The squarefree decomposition of a natural number. -/
    97â†’theorem squarefreePart_mul_squarePart (n : â„•) : n.squarefreePart * n.squarePart = n :=
    98â†’  Nat.mul_div_eq_iff_dvd.2 n.squarefreePart_dvd
    99â†’
   100â†’theorem squarefree_infinite : Set.Infinite { n : â„• | Squarefree n } :=
   101â†’  Set.Infinite.mono (fun _ hp â†¦ hp.squarefree) Nat.infinite_setOf_prime
   102â†’
   103â†’end Nat

    16â†’
    17â†’import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Squarefree

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Primitive elements of a set with respect to divisibility
    21â†’
    22â†’An element `n` is *primitive* in a set `S âٹ† â„•` if `n âˆˆ S` and no proper divisor of `n` is in `S`.
    23â†’
    24â†’This concept appears naturally when studying sequences closed under "divisor inheritance" -
    25â†’if every member `n` of a sequence has the property that `m * n` is also in the sequence
    26â†’for coprime `m`, then the primitive elements generate the entire sequence.
    27â†’
    28â†’## Main definitions
    29â†’
    30â†’* `Set.IsPrimitive`: A natural number `n` is primitive in `S` if `n âˆˆ S` and
    31â†’  `n.properDivisors` is disjoint from `S`.
    32â†’* `Set.primitives`: The set of primitive elements of `S`.
    33â†’
    34â†’## Main results
    35â†’
    36â†’* `Set.IsPrimitive.mem`: A primitive element is in the set.
    37â†’* `Set.IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_properDivisor`: Proper divisors of a primitive element are not
    38â†’  in the set.
    39â†’* `Set.one_isPrimitive_iff`: `1` is primitive in `S` iff `1 âˆˆ S`.
    40â†’* `Set.prime_isPrimitive_iff`: A prime `p` is primitive in `S` iff `p âˆˆ S` and `1 âˆ‰ S`.
    41â†’-/
    42â†’
    43â†’namespace Set
    44â†’
    45â†’/-- An element `n` is *primitive* in a set `S âٹ† â„•` if `n âˆˆ S` and no proper divisor of `n`
    46â†’is in `S`. -/
    47â†’def IsPrimitive (S : Set â„•) (n : â„•) : Prop :=
    48â†’  n âˆˆ S âˆ§ Disjoint (n.properDivisors : Set â„•) S
    49â†’
    50â†’/-- The set of primitive elements of `S`. -/
    51â†’def primitives (S : Set â„•) : Set â„• :=
    52â†’  {n | S.IsPrimitive n}
    53â†’
    54â†’theorem IsPrimitive.mem {S : Set â„•} {n : â„•} (h : S.IsPrimitive n) : n âˆˆ S := h.1
    55â†’
    56â†’theorem IsPrimitive.disjoint_properDivisors {S : Set â„•} {n : â„•} (h : S.IsPrimitive n) :
    57â†’    Disjoint (n.properDivisors : Set â„•) S := h.2
    58â†’
    59â†’theorem IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_properDivisor {S : Set â„•} {n d : â„•} (h : S.IsPrimitive n)
    60â†’    (hd : d âˆˆ n.properDivisors) : d âˆ‰ S :=
    61â†’  Set.disjoint_left.mp h.2 hd
    62â†’
    63â†’theorem IsPrimitive.not_mem_of_dvd_of_lt {S : Set â„•} {n d : â„•} (h : S.IsPrimitive n)
    64â†’    (hdvd : d âˆ£ n) (hlt : d < n) : d âˆ‰ S :=
    65â†’  h.not_mem_of_properDivisor (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mpr âں¨hdvd, hltâں©)
    66â†’
    67â†’theorem isPrimitive_iff {S : Set â„•} {n : â„•} :
    68â†’    S.IsPrimitive n â†” n âˆˆ S âˆ§ âˆ€ d âˆˆ n.properDivisors, d âˆ‰ S := by
    69â†’  simp only [IsPrimitive, Set.disjoint_left, Finset.mem_coe]
    70â†’
    71â†’theorem isPrimitive_iff' {S : Set â„•} {n : â„•} (_hn : 0 < n) :
    72â†’    S.IsPrimitive n â†” n âˆˆ S âˆ§ âˆ€ d, d âˆ£ n â†’ d < n â†’ d âˆ‰ S := by
    73â†’  rw [isPrimitive_iff]
    74â†’  constructor <;> intro âں¨hmem, hdivâں©
    75â†’  آ· exact âں¨hmem, fun d hd hlt => hdiv d (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mpr âں¨hd, hltâں©)âں©
    76â†’  آ· exact âں¨hmem, fun d hd => hdiv d (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mp hd).1 (Nat.mem_properDivisors.mp hd).2âں©
    77â†’
    78â†’theorem mem_primitives_iff {S : Set â„•} {n : â„•} : n âˆˆ S.primitives â†” S.IsPrimitive n := Iff.rfl
    79â†’
    80â†’@[simp]
    81â†’theorem primitives_subset {S : Set â„•} : S.primitives âٹ† S := fun _ h => h.mem
    82â†’
    83â†’theorem one_isPrimitive_iff {S : Set â„•} : S.IsPrimitive 1 â†” 1 âˆˆ S := by
    84â†’  simp [isPrimitive_iff, Nat.properDivisors_one]
    85â†’
    86â†’theorem prime_isPrimitive_iff {S : Set â„•} {p : â„•} (hp : p.Prime) :
    87â†’    S.IsPrimitive p â†” p âˆˆ S âˆ§ 1 âˆ‰ S := by
    88â†’  simp [isPrimitive_iff, hp.properDivisors]
    89â†’
    90â†’end Set

### Summary

The codebase contains comprehensive formalizations of:
1. **Arithmetic Progressions**: Full definitions and freeness predicates
2. **Prime Number Theory**: Prime gaps, Beurling primes, Wall-Sun-Sun primes, Lucas-Wieferich primes
3. **Number Theoretic Functions**: Factorization, squarefreeness, k-fullness, primitive elements
4. **Additive Combinatorics**: Sum-free sets, Sidon sets, additive bases, convolutions
5. **Completeness Conditions**: Additive completeness and strong completeness

While there is no direct "Goldbach" conjecture definition found, the infrastructure supports the necessary concepts for such problems through the prime, sum, and representation definitions.
